subject: Re: warp reconstructs only a region of my tomograms
body:
hi Roan,

Such issues can arise if your entries for unbinned tomogram dimensions in 
WARP (when processing tomostar files) are not consistent with the 
dimensions of the tilt-images. 

For example,
If your tilt images are 3710 by 3838 (K2 Camera) and tilt-axis angle is 
close to 90 degrees, then your unbinned tomogram dimensions should be 3838 
by 3710 by (1500 or 1200 or 2000 which is the thickness of tomogram)
If your tilt images were acquired in super-resolution mode and you want to 
bin them by 2, then you should select 1 in Bin option but set the unbinned 
tomograms dimensions to be the same of superResolution images (for example 
this could be 4092*2 by 5760*2 by 1500*2 (thickness) for K3 camera). 

HTH,
Cheers,
Digvijay




On Wednesday, December 13, 2023 at 6:23:53 AM UTC-8 roan...@gmail.com wrote:

> Dear all,
>
> I've been reconstructing my tomogram with Warp, but when I inspect my 
> tomogram with Imod it shows me only a small region out of my tomogram. 
> Before i've been doing everything with warp, made stacks in IMOD and 
> aligned those with AreTomo. In all those steps I can see my full tomogram. 
> During reconstruction it seems like, it is reconstructing just the upper 
> left corner. I tried the reconstruction several times and with different 
> bins, but that didn't change the output. 
> Did someone else had this issue before, or may have an idea what went 
> wrong?
>
> Thanks a lot
> Roan
>
